questions.question_text: A salesperson creates a flyer advertising a listing using only their own name. This advertisement is:
questions.correct_answer: A violation — it must include the broker's name
distractors: Acceptable if the salesperson has permission
Legal if the property address is included
Permitted for open house flyers only
questions.explanation: Under 254 CMR 3.00(9)(b), salespersons are prohibited from advertising the purchase, sale, rental or exchange of real property under their own name at all — advertising is done by and in the name of the employing broker. All advertisements must include the name of the real estate broker (the licensed brokerage entity or its properly filed d/b/a).

questions.question_text: An ad reads: 'Beautiful 3BR colonial for sale — call 555-1234.' No company name appears. This is known as a:
questions.explanation: A 'blind ad' is one without broker identification, and these are prohibited in Massachusetts. All advertising must include the name of the real estate broker (the licensed brokerage entity or its properly filed d/b/a).

questions.question_text: A licensee advertises a property at $450,000, but the seller authorized a list price of $475,000. This practice is:
questions.explanation: Advertising a property at a price other than the price the owner authorized is false and misleading advertising, prohibited by 254 CMR 3.00(9), and can constitute a substantial misrepresentation under MGL c.112 §87AAA. There is no separate 'price rule' — the general ban on false or misleading advertising covers it.

questions.question_text: Which element must appear in ALL real estate advertisements in Massachusetts?
questions.explanation: All advertising must include the name of the real estate broker (the licensed brokerage entity or its properly filed d/b/a). License numbers, MLS numbers, and assessed values are not required advertising elements.

questions.question_text: A broker posts a yard sign on a listing that shows only a team name and phone number, not the brokerage name. This is:
questions.correct_answer: A violation — must include the broker's name
distractors: Acceptable if the team is well-known
Legal if the website has the broker name
questions.explanation: All advertising, including yard signs, must include the name of the real estate broker (the licensed brokerage entity or its properly filed d/b/a). Team names alone do not satisfy this requirement.

questions.question_text: A licensee creates a Facebook page to market listings. What must the page include?
distractors: A disclaimer that it is not official
questions.explanation: Social media profiles and pages used for real estate must include broker information, the same as any traditional advertising. The same disclosure rules apply to all media.

questions.question_text: How do Massachusetts advertising rules for online platforms compare to rules for print advertising?
questions.correct_answer: The same disclosure rules apply to both
distractors: Online ads have relaxed disclosure rules
Print ads require more disclosures than online
Online ads only need a website link to disclosures
questions.explanation: Internet and social media advertising must comply with the same disclosure rules as traditional advertising. Broker identification must be clearly displayed in all online ads.

questions.question_text: A salesperson creates a real estate website featuring their listings. The website must prominently display:
distractors: Only the salesperson's personal brand name
questions.explanation: All real estate advertising, including websites, must include the name of the real estate broker (the licensed brokerage entity or its properly filed d/b/a). This is the same requirement that applies to print ads, signs, and social media.

questions.question_text: A licensee posts a property video on YouTube showing only their personal brand, with no mention of their brokerage. This is:
distractors: Acceptable because videos are entertainment, not advertising
questions.explanation: Any real estate advertising on any platform — including YouTube videos — must include the broker's name. Omitting it, even in video format, constitutes a prohibited blind ad.

questions.question_text: A salesperson's Instagram bio says 'Boston Real Estate Expert' but does not name their brokerage. They use this profile to market listings. This is:
distractors: Compliant if they only post personal content
Acceptable since bios have limited space
Permitted if a link goes to the brokerage site
questions.explanation: Social media profiles used for real estate must include broker information. A bio marketing real estate services without naming the brokerage violates the advertising rules.
